mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly supporting higher equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS levels, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in retail sales exceeding 1.5 percent signals an immediate surge in consumer spending that boosts near-term corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of instances where retail sales rise above the 1.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll employment growth and a declining unemployment rate indicate a tightening labor market that expands wage pressures and corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a twenty-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls, with a walk-forward Sharpe ratio below 0.5 over triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase triggers inflationary expectations and anticipated monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in over 60% of triggered instances during late-cycle expansions, or the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL exceeds 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels above 275 reflect entrenched inflation that forces central banks to accelerate rate hikes, raising discount rates and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 275, or the signal shows no incremental predictive value over baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases aggregate corporate earnings and cash flow generation, creating a fundamental tailwind that produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a sixty-day horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with GDPC1 growth rates over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent rise in the unemployment rate and a decline in housing starts signals deteriorating economic momentum and rising recession probability, causing investors to sell equities and generating negative S&P 500 returns over five days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 0.1% MoM and HOUST crosses below -5% MoM
failure_condition: The combined deterioration signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns, with positive outcomes occurring in over 65% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth lags behind consumer price inflation, supply bottlenecks constrain output and profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over twenty days during supply-shock regimes.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL MoM growth exceeds INDPRO MoM growth
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 55% of periods where inflation outpaces industrial production, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.
